name: deel
description: Read Deel contracts, workers, invoices, and timesheets for global payroll and contractor management, with optional timesheet submission

envVars.name: DEEL_API_TOKEN
envVars.description: Deel API access token (personal or organization token, generated in the Deel app under Apps & Integrations > Developer)

tools.name: List Contracts
tools.description: List contracts in the Deel account, optionally filtered by contract type

types.description: Filter by contract type, e.g. payg_tasks, payg_milestones, pay_as_you_go_time_based, eor

limit.description: Number of contracts to return per page

after_cursor.description: Pagination cursor returned by a previous page

tools.name: Get Contract
tools.description: Retrieve full details of a single contract by ID

tools.name: List People
tools.description: List people (workers) in the organization with their employment details

hiring_statuses.description: Filter by hiring status, e.g. active, onboarding, offboarding, inactive

limit.description: Number of people to return per page

offset.description: Index of the first record to return (offset pagination)

tools.name: Get Person
tools.description: Retrieve a single person (worker) with their employments, manager, and direct reports

personId.description: Deel person ID (UUID, from List People)

tools.name: List Invoices
tools.description: List worker invoices; only paid invoices are returned unless status=all is passed

issued_from_date.description: Only invoices issued on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

issued_to_date.description: Only invoices issued before this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

status.description: Pass 'all' to return invoices in every status; otherwise only paid invoices are returned

limit.description: Number of invoices to return per page

offset.description: Index of the first record to return (offset pagination)

tools.name: List Timesheets
tools.description: List timesheet entries, optionally filtered to a single contract

contract_id.description: Only return timesheets for this contract ID

limit.description: Number of timesheets to return per page

offset.description: Index of the first record to return (offset pagination)

tools.name: Create Timesheet Entry
tools.description: Submit a timesheet entry (work performed) for a contractor contract

data.description: Timesheet entry payload with required fields: contract_id (string), description (string), date_submitted (YYYY-MM-DD), quantity (number, based on the contract's scale of work). Optional: is_auto_approved (boolean)

tools.name: Review Timesheet
tools.description: Approve or decline a submitted timesheet entry; approved hours flow onto the contractor's invoice

timesheetId.description: ID of the timesheet entry to review

data.description: Review payload with required field status ('approved' or 'declined') and optional reason (string), e.g. {"status": "approved", "reason": "Hours verified"}

prompts.title: Active contracts
prompts.prompt: List the contracts in my Deel account and summarize them by type and status.

prompts.title: Workforce overview
prompts.prompt: Show me the people in my Deel organization grouped by hiring status and country.

prompts.title: Recent invoices
prompts.prompt: List Deel invoices issued in the last month and summarize the totals.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Deel API setup

steps.title: Generate an API token
steps.description: Sign in to the Deel app (https://app.deel.com), open Apps & Integrations and go to the API access / developer section, then click Generate new token. Choose an organization token (recommended for automation; never expires and is not tied to a user) or a personal token, and select the scopes the tools need: contracts:read, people:read, accounting:read, timesheets:read, and timesheets:write if you want to submit timesheets.

steps.title: Optional: use the sandbox for testing
steps.description: Deel provides an isolated sandbox with sample data that never triggers real payments or contracts. Create a sandbox account from the developer center in the Deel platform (sandbox app at demo.letsdeel.com) and generate a sandbox token there. The sandbox API base URL is https://api-sandbox.demo.deel.com/rest/v2 — to test against it, point a copy of this connector at that host.

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Add DEEL_API_TOKEN to your .env with the generated token.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Contracts. If you get a 403, check that the token includes the required scope for the endpoint.

notes: All requests must be made over HTTPS; the token is sent as Authorization: Bearer <token>.
Personal tokens are limited to what the generating user can access and expire when that user leaves the organization; organization tokens do not expire.
This connector targets the production API (api.letsdeel.com). Sandbox and production use separate credentials.
